channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Microsoft extends Entra ID to WSL, WinGet
item.description: Microsoft has added new security features to Windows Subsystem for Linux (WSL) and the Windows Package Manager (WinGet), including integration with Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Active Directory) for identity-based access control.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: CMA gives Google’s $2B Anthropic investment the green light
item.description: It took the UK’s Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) less than a month to decide that there is no need to proceed further with a merger investigation after Google’s purchase of a $2 billion stake in Anthropic. In a statement released on Oct.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The 3 next big things in robotics and automation for 2024
item.description: Cars still can’t drive themselves everywhere a human can. Robots can’t replace every human manufacturing worker. But those stubborn facts should not distract us from the interesting and creative work being done to expand the ability of robots and drones to help humans do their jobs.
item.dc:creator: Rob Pegoraro, Fast Company

item.title: The 6 next big things in enterprise technology for 2024
item.description: Some of the AI tools with the greatest impact to perform useful work don’t go out of their way to call attention to themselves.
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: The 11 next big things in AI and data innovations for 2024
item.description: The AI big bang that first caught the world’s attention with ChatGPT is sending energy and innovation out in many directions. Large language models aren’t just chatting and summarizing text: Now they’re also reasoning through problems with increasing autonomy.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Sweeping VA reform bill with pay flexibility for health care workers passes House
item.description: Lawmakers axed language in the House-passed Dole Act that set higher standards for the VA to resume the rollout of its troubled new Electronic Health Record. The post Sweeping VA reform bill with pay flexibility for health care workers passes House first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jory Heckman, Federal News Network

item.title: Cato Networks adds TLS inspection capabilities to SASE platform
item.description: Secure access service edge provider Cato Networks today announced it added transport layer security inspection capabilities to its SASE platform to enable customers to inspect TLS traffic more easily and quickly.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Data breach exposes over 56 million clothing store customers
item.description: Breach site confirmed 56,904,909 Hot Topic users' data leaked online. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says the company's silence makes matters even worse.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Microsoft pitches AI 'agents' that can perform tasks on their own at Ignite 2024
item.description: Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella told customers at a conference in Chicago on Tuesday that the company is teaching a new set of artificial intelligence tools how to “act on our behalf across our work and life.”.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Map an AI strategy that’s yours, not SAP’s
item.description: As enthusiasm for AI and generative AI mounts, creating a winning AI strategy to help reduce operating costs and increase efficiency is easily topping the priority list for IT executives. There’s little question businesses are ready to reap the rewards of AI.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Microsoft and Atom Computing claim breakthrough in reliable quantum computing
item.description: Microsoft and Atom Computing have announced what they claim is a significant step forward in reliable quantum computing, unveiling a commercial quantum machine built with 24 entangled logical qubits.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Luz verde al nuevo laboratorio de innovación en APIs para Open Gateway
item.description: MasOrange, Telefónica, Vodafone Global y el centro de investigación i2CAT se han unido para poner en marcha el primer laboratorio de APIs Open Gateway multioperador de Europa.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Modernizing Architecture Review Boards for Collaboration and Innovation
item.description: Learn how to modernize architecture review boards, prioritize collaboration, and drive innovation with AI-driven tools and best practices.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Judge tosses Huckabee lawsuit against Meta over ads suggesting he endorsed marijuana gummies
item.description: WILMINGTON, Del. (AP) — A federal judge in Delaware on Monday dismissed a lawsuit filed by former Arkansas Gov. Mike Huckabee against social media giant Meta over advertisements using his name and image to sell CBD products.
item.dc:creator: RANDALL CHASE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: How a wrong Google search can compromise your data and bring law enforcement calling
item.description: Wrong Google searches can not only compromise your device and personal data. They can also bring law enforcement to your doorstep. Hackers are targeting Google searches.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A challenge to recreate how the International Space Station was built
item.description: A new challenge looks to digitally recreate the planning of each module of the International Space Station. The post A challenge to recreate how the International Space Station was built first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Eric White, Federal News Network

item.title: For November, Patch Tuesday includes three Windows zero-day fixes
item.description: Microsoft’s November Patch Tuesday release addresses 89 vulnerabilities in Windows, SQL Server, .NET and Microsoft Office — and three zero-day vulnerabilities (CVE-2024-43451, CVE-2024-49019 and CVE-2024-49039) that mean a patch now recommendation for Windows platforms.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft’s anticompetitive behavior weakens its customers’ cybersecurity
item.description: If left unchecked, Microsoft’s practices will become the industry standard, clearing the path for other providers to harm customers and weaken their defenses. The post Microsoft’s anticompetitive behavior weakens its customers’ cybersecurity first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: JDK 24: The new features in Java 24
item.description: In the wake of Java Development Kit (JDK) 23, which arrived September 17, work now focuses on the planned successor release, JDK 24, which has ballooned to 21 proposed features.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Solar-powered aircraft achieves groundbreaking 22-hour autonomous flight
item.description: Skydweller is the world's largest unmanned solar-powered aircraft that can stay airborne for weeks, and even months, without refueling.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NASA Establishes New Class of Astrophysics Missions, Selects Studies
item.description: NASA selects two proposals for missions to observe X-ray and far-infrared wavelengths of light from space for additional review The post NASA Establishes New Class of Astrophysics Missions, Selects Studies first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Eric White, Federal News Network

item.title: Elon Musk says he and Trump have 'mandate to delete' regulations. Ethics laws could limit Musk role
item.description: In picking billionaire Elon Musk to be “our cost cutter” for the U.S. government, President-elect Donald Trump won't be the first American president to empower a business tycoon to look for ways to dramatically cut federal regulations. President Ronald Reagan tapped J.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and TOM KRISHER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Chinese hackers target Tibetan websites in malware attack, cybersecurity group says
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — A hacking group that is believed to be Chinese state-sponsored has compromised two websites with ties to the Tibetan community in an attack meant to install malware on users' computers, according to findings released Wednesday by a private cybersecurity firm.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING, Associated Press, ABC 4
